programmes.show_description: The Jump-up Boys. Stars Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie dons a white coat and takes a real job at last. But hearing that Budgie has gone straight, Charles suspects foul play.
1957. Crime. Directed by John Gilling. Stars Victor Mature, Anita Ekberg & Trevor Howard. An FBI agent will stop at nothing to catch the narcotics kingpin responsible for his sister's death.
1954. Drama. Directed by Lewis R. Foster. Stars David Niven, Martha Hyer & Hal Baylor. A young lady mistakes a hotel desk clerk for an oil millionaire she'd once met, and he decides to play along.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Living on a Volcano
programmes.show_description: 1967. A remarkable look at Ascension Island, an isolated volcanic island in the equatorial waters of the South Atlantic Ocean; home to many families, who sometimes work there for up to two years.
Silent Partner. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E11)
Another Neighbour. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, Jean Willes & Susan Hart. The more prominent residents of Beverly Hills become more than a little affected when they sample Granny's spring tonic.
1949. Thriller. Directed by Oswald Mitchell. Stars John Stuart, Henry Oscar & Marie Burke. A psychic visits the home of his old friend and his wife but soon makes an unsettling discovery.
1953. War Time Drama. Lilli will not marry her love, an American reporter, until she becomes a star. Starring Lisa Daniely, Hugh McDermott and Sid James.
1938. War. Directed by Zoltan Korda. Stars Sabu, Raymond Massey & Roger Livesey. When the Khan of Tokot is murdered, his killer, the evil Prince Ghul, plots an uprising against British rule.
1954. Adventure. Directed by Tay Garnett. Stars Alan Ladd, Patricia Medina & Peter Cushing. A commoner disguises himself as the black knight to reveal those trying to overthrow King Arthur.
1957. Drama. Directed by David Eady. Stars James Hayter, Earl Cameron & David Hemmings. A murder suspect on the run must depend on strangers to help prove his innocence and clear his name.
The Raiders. 1956. Stars Alec Clunes, Jane Griffiths & Peter Hammond. New Providence is in danger from pirate raiders led by Charles Vane, leaving Governor Rogers to face a tough decision.
The Ordeal. 1976. Stars Jean Anderson, Patrick O'Connell & Richard Easton. The Riley family faces sobering news. Paul admits his feelings and gives April a final chance to save their marriage.
Special Edition. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Richard Bennet & Donald Bisset. A gang of career criminals believe they have the perfect alibi when they carry out a payroll robbery in Fleet Street.
1956. Drama. Directed by Jack Lee. Stars Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch & Kenji Takaki. While suffering the hardships of being a prisoner of war, Jean meets and befriends captive Australian Joe.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout the end of March and the beginning of April.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. This episode focuses on a wonderful tribute to Margaret Leighton.
Wheels/Grayling Fishing. 1981. Jack Hargreaves takes his strawberry cart to Mr. White the wheelwright for repairs. He then goes to the river Kennet for a spot of fishing for grayling.
1957. Night Crossing. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Starring Russell Napier, John Serret and Andre Maranne. The quiet of an idyllic port town is shattered when a body is found on the beach.
Let's Make a Killing. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Shirley Anne Field & Clifford Rose. Suspicions grow about Burton's ''big deal''. Tony and Ray fly to Africa and uncover something significant.
1976. Drama. Director: George P Cosmatos. Stars Sophia Loren, Richard Harris, Burt Lancaster & Martin Sheen. Passengers of a European train can't escape after a deadly virus is planted.
1982. Drama. Director: Jan Worth. Stars Sandy Ratcliff, Paul Copley & Bernice Stegers. The stories of three women: a researcher, a prostitute and a part-time switchboard operator.
Our Story so Far. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie is on the run again. But this time he has the police hot on his tail as well as Charlie. Who will catch him first?
1971. Comedy. Directed by Waris Hussein. Stars Mark Lester, Tracy Hyde & Jack Wild. Two youngsters declare to their parents they want to get married as soon as possible.
High Lonesome. Western starring Wayne Rogers, Richard Eyer & Robert Bray. Simon Kane drubs his supervisor Osgood for revealing that Kane's wife deserted him and his son.
The Case of Lady Beryl. 1954. Starring: Ronald Howard, Howard Marion-Crawford & Paulette Goddard. Lady Beryl confesses to a murder, but Sherlock Holmes is convinced that she didn't do it.
1959. Murdock. Western with Peter Breck. After his brothers are killed in a shootout, a gunfighter decides to hang up his guns and uphold the law by becoming a lawyer.
1959. Mystery. Directed by Tony Young. Stars Griffith Jones & Patricia Laffan. A writer wakes up in a strange place with a gun in his hand and a dead body, but he doesn't remember what happened.
Travel to Italy with Hughie Green. Take in the sights and sounds of places like, Pompeii, Naples and Venice.
1953. Drama. Director: Philip Leacock. Stars: Jon Whiteley, Vincent Winter, Adrienne Corri, Duncan Macrae, Jean Anderson. Two Canadian orphans 'borrow' a baby to raise as their own.
1962. Comedy. Directed by Ken Annakin. Stars Leslie Phillips, Stanley Baxter & Wilfrid Hyde-White. Petty thief, Dandy Forsdyke, joins an unofficial organisation for reforming criminals.
Ferocious Fathers. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Lancelot goes to the rescue of Urgan the Strong, whose castle is besieged by Sir Melius the Mighty and his sons.

programmes.show_name: Selsey Pavilion: The Journey So Far
programmes.show_description: 2025. Created by students of The Academy Selsey and Millstream Productions, this documentary explores the history of the Selsey Pavilion, a pre World War One Art Deco theatre & cinema.
1960. Comedy. Director Robert Asher. Stars Norman Wisdom, Edward Chapman, Terence Alexander, David Lodge. Norman Puckle joins the Navy under the same Admiral he refused to yield to in the harbour.
Do Your Worst - Part 1. 1974. Stars David Ashford, Maureen Lipman & William Mervyn. The Fulchester Rovers manager is sacked. He thinks the players conspired and poorly performed deliberately.
Do Your Worst - Part 2. 1974. Stars Jill Kerman, Roy Holder & Ronald Lewis. Skelhorn's ex-wife had overheard three players discuss giving away penalties. She passed this onto the club manager.
Do Your Worst - Part 3. 1974 Stars Maureen Lipman & David Wood. Prosecutor Sara Lewis highlights discipline issues within the team. Chairman Brian Martindale gives his reasons for sacking Gruda.
2024. Directed by Evelyn Schels. He fled Third Reich and antisemitism for refuge in Hollywood. This is the journey of enigmatic actor Peter Lorre, known for his villainous roles in over 80 films.
1965. Comedy. Directed by Jay Lewis Stars Ronnie Barker, Peter Butterworth & Bernard Cribbins. A wordless, satirical look at British builders as they attempt to build a young couple's dream home.
Stockbridge Bypass/Angels. 1994. Michael Aspel introduces tales of the paranormal, told through eyewitness accounts and reconstructions. Today he asks whether guardian angels really exist.
The Trojan Horse. 1973. Stars Richard Easton, Anna Fox & Brown Derby. The stand-off between the Hammond's and Carter continues. Meanwhile, Mary continues to manipulate the lives of her children.
A Coin's Worth of Murder. 1958. Stars Donald Gray, Diana Decker & Colin Tapley. Sally begs Saber to find the truth after her fiancée's apparent suicide because Sally believes this is a murder.

programmes.show_name: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programmes.show_description: Episode 29. 1975. Ray Ellington and his Quartet perform 'I Could Have Danced All Night' and 'Tit Willow'. The Grumbleweeds and Frank Carson also make appearances.
1989. Drama. Director: Michael Caton-Jones. Stars John Hurt, Joanne Whalley & Bridget Fonda. An affair between an exotic dancer and the Minister of War shakes up the British government.
Re-Entry Forbidden. 1970. Stars John Paul, Michael McGovern & Joseph Fürst. A new nuclear-powered rocket is tested, but the slightest error could turn the vessel into a flying nuclear bomb.
